evals.name: cohort-setup-from-scratch
evals.prompt: I have a new ATLL (adult T-cell leukemia) cohort with 4 patients. Each patient has one tumor sample (existing BAM) and one normal sample (pod5 raw data only, not yet basecalled). I want to track which samples have been sorted, variant-called, and methylation-called using casetrack. The project should live at /tmp/atll_new/. Walk me through setting this up from scratch — what commands do I run in what order, and what should casetrack.toml look like?
evals.expected_output: Agent produces an ordered sequence: casetrack init, edit TOML to add analyses (samtools_sort, sniffles2, modkit_pileup) plus pod5_path on assays, casetrack schema apply, add-metadata for patients then specimens then assays with --allow-new --yes. Should NOT use 'casetrack append' for entity registration.

evals.name: record-analysis-results
evals.prompt: I just finished running samtools sort on all 6 tumor specimens in my casetrack project at /path/to/projects/project_demo. I wrote a summary TSV at /tmp/sort_summary.tsv with columns: specimen_id, sorted_bam_path, sorted_bam_size_bytes, n_reads, sort_order (one row per specimen). How do I record these results in the casetrack DB so the specimens' sort_done timestamp and sort stats columns get populated? This is the second time I've run this analysis — the first run populated some cells with earlier data.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses `casetrack append --project-dir <path> --analysis samtools_sort --results /tmp/sort_summary.tsv --overwrite`. Critically includes --overwrite (because the user noted it's a rerun). Uses --analysis samtools_sort (not add-metadata, not a different analysis name). Explains that column_prefix 'sort' from the TOML prepends to columns.

evals.name: generate-pending-samplesheet
evals.prompt: I need a Nextflow samplesheet for all the normal samples in /path/to/projects/project_demo that are ready to be basecalled with dorado but haven't been yet. One of the normals (s_demo_normal_6) is censored because its pod5 rsync isn't complete — it should be excluded. Generate the SQL query and show me the resulting CSV with columns: sample, patient, specimen, pod5_dir, genome.
evals.expected_output: Agent writes a SQL query that JOINs assays, specimens, patients; filters condition='normal', qc_status='pass' (or uses _active view), dorado_basecaller_done IS NULL. Produces a CSV with exactly 5 rows (p_demo_1 through p_demo_5 normals). Uses `casetrack query --project-dir ... --sql '...'` or the MCP tool.

evals.name: schema-migration-with-data
evals.prompt: I added 3 new columns to my casetrack.toml under [levels.assay.columns] (genome, library_kit, run_date) but now when I try to run `casetrack add-metadata --level assay --metadata new_assays.tsv` I get `sqlite3.OperationalError: no such column: genome`. The DB already has 18 assays I don't want to lose. How do I fix this without rebuilding the project?
evals.expected_output: Agent identifies the problem: TOML edits don't auto-apply to the SQLite DB. Tells user to run `casetrack schema apply --project-dir .` which runs non-destructive ALTER TABLE statements (existing data preserved). Mentions schema_v gets bumped. Should NOT suggest dropping the DB or re-init.

evals.name: rerun-after-bug-fix
evals.prompt: I just patched a bug in my modkit_callmods summary script — it was reporting n_reads as zero for everyone. All 12 specimens in project_demo are already marked modkit_callmods_done with the buggy values. I have a fresh /tmp/modkit_callmods_summary.tsv with the corrected stats (one row per specimen, keyed on specimen_id). How do I push the corrected values into the casetrack DB?
evals.expected_output: Agent uses `casetrack append --project-dir <path> --analysis modkit_callmods --results /tmp/modkit_callmods_summary.tsv --overwrite`. The --overwrite is critical — without it, fill-only behavior keeps the buggy zeros. Should NOT suggest deleting rows or running uncensor (modkit_callmods_done isn't a QC event). Should NOT use add-metadata.

evals.name: nextflow-infer-from-path-troubleshoot
evals.prompt: My Nextflow CASETRACK_REGISTER process keeps failing with `Error: --analysis is required (or infer it with --infer-from-path)`. Inside the process I'm running `casetrack append --infer-from-path --overwrite` from the directory `results/samtools_sort/20260421_hg38_test/p_demo_1/p_demo_1_tumor/`, where I just placed a TSV named `sort_summary.tsv`. My casetrack.toml has [analyses.samtools_sort] with summary_tsv = 'samtools_sort_summary.tsv'. What's wrong and how do I fix it?
evals.expected_output: Agent identifies the filename mismatch: the file on disk is `sort_summary.tsv` but the TOML declares `summary_tsv = 'samtools_sort_summary.tsv'`. --infer-from-path looks for the declared filename. Fix is to rename the file (or update the TOML). Bonus points if agent notes the path also looks valid against [layout.path_templates.specimen].

evals.name: consent-revoked-cascade
evals.prompt: Patient p_demo_3 in my project at /path/to/projects/project_demo just withdrew consent on 2026-01-15 (IRB ref: IRB-DEMO-001). I need to record this in casetrack so all their specimens and assays disappear from analysis queries automatically. How do I do this and what are the implications? Show me the exact command and explain the cascade.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses `casetrack censor --project-dir <path> --level patient --id p_demo_3 --kind consent_revoked --reason 'consent withdrawn 2026-01-15 IRB IRB-DEMO-001' --withdrawal-date 2026-01-15`. Explains _active view cascades: any specimen/assay under p_demo_3 is automatically excluded from all queries against _active. Mentions reversal requires --ethics-override --yes.

evals.name: cross-project-cohort-overlap
evals.prompt: I have 3 separate casetrack projects on this machine using overlapping patient cohorts: project_demo (ONT WGS), and hypothetically project_demo_b (RNA-seq) and project_demo_c (CITE-seq). I want to find which patient_ids exist in all 3 projects. How would I do this with casetrack? Walk me through the discovery and the queries.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses `mcp__casetrack__casetrack_list_projects` first to enumerate registered projects, then uses `mcp__casetrack__casetrack_query` against each project to get patient_ids. Notes that the comparison is done client-side (each casetrack project has its own DB; there's no cross-project JOIN built in). Suggests scripting it via Python or shell. Should NOT invent a `casetrack cohort-overlap` command if it doesn't exist; should mention `casetrack cohort` if it knows it.

evals.name: register-cohort-from-wide-sheet
evals.prompt: I have a single wide TSV (cohort.tsv) with one row per assay and columns for patient_id, specimen_id, assay_id, sex, condition, tissue_site, assay_type, and pod5_path — about 24 rows for 6 patients × 2 specimens × 2 assays. I just ran `casetrack init` on a fresh project and my casetrack.toml has the required columns declared on each level. What's the fastest way to load all three levels (patients, specimens, assays) into the DB in one shot?
evals.expected_output: Agent uses `casetrack register-cohort --project-dir . --samplesheet cohort.tsv --dry-run` to preview, then runs the same command without `--dry-run` to commit. Identifies this as a v0.10 / proposal 0012 feature. Notes that the entire load happens in a single transaction — patients, specimens, and assays land atomically (no partial state if any row violates a constraint). Should NOT recommend three separate `add-metadata --level patient/specimen/assay` calls as the primary answer (still legal but slower and non-atomic). Should NOT use `append` for entity loading.

evals.name: reference-version-bump-staleness
evals.prompt: I just edited casetrack.toml to bump my dbSNP reference version from 156 to 157 (the path is the same, just a newer file in place). I ran `casetrack schema apply` to register the new version. Now I want to know: which of my analyses recorded against the old dbSNP version are now stale and need to be re-run? Show me how to query this.
evals.expected_output: Agent uses `casetrack references --project-dir . --stale-only` to list reference-stale outputs, or queries the `_reference_usage` view directly (`SELECT * FROM _reference_usage WHERE state = 'STALE'`), or queries `_cohort_artifacts WHERE ref_stale = TRUE` for cohort-level artifacts. Identifies this as proposal 0010 territory. Mentions that ref_stale is read-time and three-state (`fresh` / `STALE` / `untracked`) — staleness is re-derived on every query, not stored. Notes that ref_stale is orthogonal to the input-stale flag (a sample-censor cascade) and the derived_stale flag (transitive across artifact-to-artifact lineage from 0011). Should NOT suggest manually re-running everything; should focus on the specific stale set. Should NOT suggest editing the DB or maintaining a version-history table.

evals.name: dmr-cohort-artifact-with-region-scope
evals.prompt: I just finished a DSS DMR analysis comparing 4 tumor specimens (T1-T4) against 4 normal specimens (N1-N4) restricted to the EPDnew promoter set. The output is a BED file at /data/processed/hg38/cohort/dmr_promoters.bed.gz. My casetrack.toml already declares `[references.promoters_EPDnew]` with path and version='2026-04-14'. How do I register this DMR run as a cohort artifact such that (a) the tumor/normal design is captured per input and (b) the artifact gets automatically flagged as ref_stale if I later bump the promoters_EPDnew reference?
evals.expected_output: Agent uses `casetrack append-cohort --project-dir . --analysis dss_dmr --run-tag <YYYYMMDD>_hg38_promoters --path /data/processed/hg38/cohort/dmr_promoters.bed.gz --region-scope promoters_EPDnew --inputs T1:tumor,T2:tumor,T3:tumor,T4:tumor,N1:normal,N2:normal,N3:normal,N4:normal`. Identifies this as v0.11 / proposal 0013 territory. The critical insight: because `--region-scope promoters_EPDnew` matches a registered `ref_key`, casetrack auto-captures a cohort-scope `reference_usage` row, so the artifact's `ref_stale` flag tracks the panel's version automatically — no separate `--uses-references` flag is needed for that ref. The `assay_id:role` syntax in `--inputs` records the tumor/normal contrast design (descriptive only, not staleness-bearing). Should NOT suggest using `append` (sample-level). May note that listing `promoters_EPDnew` in both `--region-scope` and `--uses-references` is redundant but harmless (idempotent dedup at the `reference_usage` level).

evals.name: cohort-artifact-stale-from-censored-input
evals.prompt: Last month I registered a joint VCF via `casetrack append-cohort --analysis joint_genotyping --run-tag 20260415_hg38_v1 --inputs A1,A2,A3,A4,A5,A6 --path /data/processed/hg38/cohort/joint.vcf.gz`. Today, assay A3 turned out to have a basecalling quality issue and I censored it with `casetrack censor --level assay --id A3 --kind qc_fail`. Is the joint VCF artifact now stale, and how do I confirm? What's the right next step?
evals.expected_output: Agent runs `casetrack cohort-artifacts --project-dir . --stale-only` (or without `--stale-only` to inspect all) and confirms the joint VCF now shows `stale=True` with A3 listed in its censored inputs. Explains that input-staleness is read-time, derived live from the QC/consent cascade — nothing was stored at censor time; the staleness flag is re-derived on every read of the `_cohort_artifacts` view. Notes that the existing artifact row stays in the audit trail; the recommended next step is a new `append-cohort` run with a fresh `run_tag` (e.g. `20260520_hg38_v2`) excluding A3, so both runs coexist in history. Should NOT suggest manually editing the artifact row or deleting it from `cohort_artifacts`. Should NOT confuse this with `ref_stale` (reference-driven, separate flag).
